_meta.label: NNT/NNH Reference — BNYS Evidence Metrics
_meta.description: Number Needed to Treat (NNT) and Number Needed to Harm (NNH) for high-evidence BNYS interventions. Sources: RCTs, Cochrane reviews, meta-analyses. NNT = patients treated per 1 benefiting. NNH = patients treated per 1 harmed. null = harm not established in literature.
_meta.author: Dr. Vikranth · BNYS

interventions.name: Berberine
interventions.dose: 500 mg three times daily with meals
interventions.outcome: HbA1c reduction ≥0.5% or FBG reduction ≥1 mmol/L
interventions.evidence_note: Meta-analysis of 14 RCTs; comparable efficacy to metformin 500 mg TID

interventions.name: Ashwagandha (Withania somnifera)
interventions.outcome: Serum cortisol reduction ≥15% + PSS score improvement
interventions.evidence_note: Double-blind RCT (Chandrasekhar et al., 2012); n=64

interventions.name: Yoga (Hatha/Integrated)
interventions.evidence_note: Cochrane review (Cramer et al., 2014); 17 RCTs included

interventions.name: Fenugreek (Trigonella foenum-graecum)
interventions.dose: 10 g soaked seeds/day or 1 g hydroalcoholic extract
interventions.evidence_note: Systematic review of 12 RCTs (Neelakantan et al., 2014)

interventions.name: Curcumin (Turmeric extract)
interventions.indication: Knee Osteoarthritis — pain and functional improvement
interventions.evidence_note: Meta-analysis of 8 RCTs (Daily et al., 2016)

interventions.name: Ginger (Zingiber officinale)
interventions.dose: 1–1.5 g/day dried root equivalent (4 divided doses)
interventions.evidence_note: Cochrane review (Matthews et al., 2015); safe in first trimester

interventions.name: Pranayama (Slow breathing/Nadi Shodhana)
interventions.evidence_note: RCT (Sharma et al., 2014); HRV and anxiety scores improved

interventions.name: Mindfulness-Based Stress Reduction (MBSR)
interventions.indication: Chronic Pain — pain intensity and quality of life
interventions.evidence_note: JAMA Internal Medicine meta-analysis (Goyal et al., 2014)

interventions.name: Hydrotherapy (Balneotherapy)
interventions.indication: Fibromyalgia — pain, fatigue, and tender points
interventions.evidence_note: Cochrane review (Langhorst et al., 2013); 10 RCTs

interventions.name: Amla (Phyllanthus emblica / Indian Gooseberry)
interventions.indication: Dyslipidaemia — LDL and total cholesterol reduction
interventions.outcome: LDL reduction ≥10 mg/dL or total cholesterol ≥15 mg/dL
interventions.evidence_note: RCT (Gopa et al., 2012); significant lipid-lowering vs placebo

interventions.name: Garlic (Allium sativum)
interventions.nnh_event: Halitosis, body odour, GI discomfort, antiplatelet effect
interventions.evidence_note: Cochrane review (Stabler et al., 2012); 7 RCTs

interventions.name: Flaxseed (Linum usitatissimum)
interventions.evidence_note: Meta-analysis (Pan et al., 2009); greatest effect in hypercholesterolaemic patients

interventions.name: Yoga (Integrated) for Diabetes
interventions.indication: Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus — HbA1c and FBG control
interventions.outcome: HbA1c reduction ≥0.5% or FBG reduction ≥10 mg/dL
interventions.evidence_note: Systematic review (Innes & Selfe, 2016); 25 RCTs; adjunct to standard care

interventions.name: Gymnema sylvestre
interventions.indication: Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus — glucose and HbA1c
interventions.evidence_note: RCT (Baskaran et al., 1990); significant beta-cell regeneration marker changes

interventions.name: Yoga Nidra (Yogic Sleep)
interventions.indication: Chronic Insomnia — sleep onset latency and quality
interventions.evidence_note: RCT (Kumar, 2010); significant improvement in sleep architecture

interventions.name: Psyllium Husk (Isabgol)
interventions.outcome: LDL reduction ≥5% OR bowel frequency normalisation
interventions.evidence_note: FDA-approved heart health claim; meta-analysis (Anderson et al., 2000)

interventions.name: Triphala
interventions.dose: 5 g churna or 500 mg extract at bedtime
interventions.evidence_note: RCT (Laxmivandana et al., 2018); well-tolerated across age groups

interventions.name: Neem (Azadirachta indica)
interventions.dose: 4–5 g neem leaf powder/day or standardised extract 500 mg
interventions.evidence_note: Pilot RCTs; preclinical evidence robust; limited human trial data

interventions.name: Surya Namaskar (Sun Salutation)
interventions.indication: Overweight/Obesity — BMI and body fat reduction
interventions.outcome: BMI reduction ≥1 kg/m² or body fat ≥2%
interventions.evidence_note: RCT (Bhutkar et al., 2011); significant anthropometric improvements

interventions.name: Whole-Food Plant-Based Diet (WFPB)
interventions.indication: Cardiovascular Risk Reduction — LDL, BP, weight
interventions.dose: Ad libitum WFPB diet (no added oils, refined grains, or processed foods)
interventions.outcome: ≥1 risk factor improved: LDL ≥10%, SBP ≥5 mmHg, or BMI ≥1 kg/m²
interventions.evidence_note: BROAD trial (Wright et al., 2017); 65 participants; NNT calculated from composite endpoint

interventions.name: Cold Water Immersion (Hydrotherapy)
interventions.dose: 10–15 min immersion at 10–15°C within 1 h post-exercise
interventions.outcome: VAS muscle soreness reduction ≥2/10 at 24 h
interventions.evidence_note: Cochrane review (Bleakley et al., 2012); 17 RCTs

interventions.name: Kunjal Kriya (Vaman Dhauti)
interventions.nnh_event: Aspiration risk if technique improper; contraindicated in peptic ulcer
interventions.evidence_note: Observational study + clinical naturopathic evidence; limited RCT data

interventions.name: Intermittent Fasting (16:8 or 5:2 Protocol)
interventions.indication: Metabolic Syndrome — weight, insulin resistance, triglycerides
interventions.dose: 16-h fast/8-h eating window daily OR 500 kcal restriction 2 days/week
interventions.evidence_note: Meta-analysis (Harris et al., 2018); 41 trials; equivalent to continuous energy restriction

interventions.name: Mud Therapy (Pelotherapy)
interventions.indication: Rheumatoid Arthritis — pain and morning stiffness
interventions.dose: Local mud packs at affected joints 30 min/day, 5×/week
interventions.outcome: DAS28 score reduction ≥1.2 or VAS pain reduction ≥30%
interventions.evidence_note: RCT (Forestier et al., 2010); spa/balneotherapy in inflammatory arthritis

interventions.name: Holy Basil / Tulsi (Ocimum tenuiflorum)
interventions.indication: Metabolic Stress — blood glucose, cortisol, cognitive fatigue
interventions.outcome: FBG reduction ≥5 mg/dL AND subjective stress score improvement
interventions.evidence_note: Double-blind RCT (Agrawal et al., 1996) + cognitive function RCT (Bhattacharya, 2015)

interventions.name: Moringa (Moringa oleifera)
interventions.dose: 7 g leaf powder/day or 500 mg standardised extract
interventions.evidence_note: Pilot RCTs (2016–2020); strong preclinical evidence; human data emerging
